items.title: National Anti‑Drugs Agency reports 81pc recovery rate among 26,852 clients nationwide
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, March 22 — A total of 21,794 clients who underwent community-based treatment and rehabilitation programmes, nationwide, achieved permanent recovery status last year. National Anti-Drugs Agency (AADK) director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh said that the recovery rate reached 81 per cent, out of the total 26,852 clients evaluated. Elaborating further, Ruslin said that last year, a total of 1,500 drug addicts voluntarily reported to the Narcotics Addiction Rehabilitation Centres (Puspen) to undergo treatment and rehabilitation programmes.

items.title: Human Resources minister hails young Malaysians’ international success at Bangkok Innovation Awards
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 22 — Four Malaysian students excelled at the Bangkok Innovation Awards, winning two gold medals and second place in the Engineering category for primary schools. The four students are Ilaneyan Yuvarajan and M. Theerchanna from Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan Tamil (SJKT) KO Sarangapany, as well as Ilanarun Yuvarajan and M. Elamaaran from Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Sultan Badlishah. Ramanan, who is also the Member of Parliament for Sungai Buloh, expressed appreciation to the students’ parents who were willing to sacrifice their time to travel to Bangkok to give moral support.

items.title: Pahang police: Contractor transferred funds 16 times before realising ‘marinasand’ investment was a scam
items.summary: Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the victim, a 34-year-old man, saw an investment advertisement on Google on February 18 through a website known as “marinasand”. “The victim then registered and participated in the investment, which promised returns of 10 per cent on the total investment,” he said in a statement today. He said the victim had made 16 money transfer transactions using personal savings as well as company funds into five different bank accounts from February 18 to March 19, 2026.

items.title: Motorcyclist lies motionless after being hit by car on SUKE highway
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A rider is seen lying motionless on the road after a blue car rammed into him on the Sungai Besi–Ulu Kelang Elevated Expressway (SUKE) in a video that has gone viral on social media. Another car then speeds past the red car before crashing into the motorcyclist. The rider is flung onto the car's bonnet before being thrown onto the road.

items.title: King, Queen grace Aidilfitri open house at Johor's Istana Besar
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, graced the Aidilfitri open house at Istana Besar here today. The King was accompanied by Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia; the Regent of Johor Tunku Mahkota Ismail; his consort Che' Puan Mahkota Khaleeda Johor; and, Their Royal Highnesses' children. More than 20,000 people of various races attended the Istana Johor open house, as members of the public began queuing outside the palace gates as early as 6.30am.

items.title: Crowds queue from dawn as Sultan Ibrahim hosts Aidilfitri open house in Johor Bahru
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 22 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, graced the Aidilfitri open house at Istana Besar here today. The King was accompanied by Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia; the Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail; his consort Che’ Puan Mahkota Khaleeda Bustamam; and Their Royal Highnesses’ children. Queen Raja Zarith Sofiah shares handshakes and smiles with the crowd at the Johor palace Aidilfitri open house today.

items.title: Thai authorities halt sand dredging activity on Malaysian side of Golok River
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Thai authorities have halted sand dredging activities along the Malaysian side of Golok River following complaints raised through bilateral border coordination channels. Sungai Golok district operations centre director Supeeyan Daemokleng said the action was taken on March 17 after the centre received a report on the activity from Narathiwat authorities. "The activity sparked concern among Malaysian authorities.

items.title: CPO futures seen trading higher next week amid rising energy prices
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Crude palm oil (CPO) futures on Bursa Malaysia Derivatives are expected to trade with an upward bias next week amid the conflict in West Asia. Iceberg X Sdn Bhd proprietary trader David Ng said the conflict has pushed energy prices higher, lending support to sentiment on palm oil. "We see prices ranging between RM4,450 and RM4,680 (per tonne) next week," he told Bernama.

items.title: On Hari Raya duty, maritime personnel sacrifice family celebrations
items.summary: Selangor Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) officers sacrifice time with family and festive meals as they continue duties throughout the celebrations. Commanding officer of KM Danga, Lieutenant Commander Muhammad Rasul Abu Bakar, said the vessel has been deployed under ongoing maritime operations since March 17 and will remain on duty through the festive period. Rasul said being away during Hari Raya was emotionally challenging for his family.

items.title: Energy supply stable despite global uncertainty
items.summary: KUCHING: The Federal Government has given an assurance that the country's energy supply remains stable despite global uncertainties following conflicts in West Asia, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. "So far, the country's energy supply remains at a stable level due to early planning and diversification of supply sources. "The government, through relevant ministries, is closely monitoring water supply together with water providers in each state to ensure sufficient supply.

items.title: Musk launches 'Terafab' project to make own AI chips
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Elon Musk announced Saturday a plan to make chips for artificial intelligence, robotics and data centers in space, in the latest bold project by the world's richest person. The "Terafab", a manufacturing facility based near Austin, Texas, will aim to produce one terawatt of computing power per year, Musk said. Musk said the project would be run jointly by his electric-vehicle firm Tesla and his rocket company SpaceX.

items.title: Geopolitical Fuel for China's Electric Ambitions: A Malaysian Perspective
items.summary: THE first time the West decided to stir things up in the Middle East in the 1970s, it led to a crisis in oil supply that saw pumps bled dry and queues forming for blocks. History repeated itself when the Koreans took advantage of erratic oil prices during America's second venture into the Middle East at the start of the millennium. Every time oil prices reach record levels, fuel efficiency improves - not necessarily to save the planet, but to save our pockets.

items.title: Heatwave grips parts of country as northeast monsoon nears end
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is entering the tail end of the Northeast Monsoon, a transitional phase marked by reduced rainfall and rising temperatures that have triggered heatwaves in several areas. Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM) Institute of Climate Change senior lecturer Dr Ricky Kemarau said the shift is being felt as several locations record unusually high daily temperatures, raising public health concerns. He said the current conditions align with climate patterns typical of the monsoon's end.

items.title: Iran threatens Middle East infrastructure after Trump ultimatum
items.summary: TEHRAN : Iran has threatened to attack key Middle East infrastructure if US President Donald Trump fulfills a vow to "obliterate" the republic's power plants unless the Strait of Hormuz is reopened within 48 hours. Trump ratcheted up pressure on Tehran on Sunday, announcing a countdown over the de facto blockade of the crucial trade route. Israel said it launched a fresh wave of strikes on Tehran on Sunday in response.

items.title: Teen pillion rider killed in Banting motorcycle crash
items.summary: BANTING: An 18-year-old pillion rider was killed and the rider injured, in a road crash at Km2, Jalan Olak Lempit-KLIA here. Kuala Langat police chief Superintendent Mohd Akmalrizal Radzi said the district Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department received information involving a red Honda motorcycle about 1.15pm yesterday. "The motorcycle then scraped against a road barrier, causing both the rider and pillion rider to be thrown onto the road," he said in a statement.

items.title: Elon Musk offers to pay TSA salaries amid budget battle and airport line‑ups
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Billionaire entrepreneur Elon Musk, the world's richest person, said on Saturday he would cover the paychecks of US Transportation Security Administration officers amid their second unpaid work stoppage in six months as a protracted federal funding lapse persists. The budget impasse over funding for the TSA's parent agency, the Department of Homeland Security, is in its fifth week. DHS funding timelines remain uncertain.

items.title: Malaysia’s energy supply remains stable despite West Asia tensions, says Fadillah
items.summary: KUCHING, March 22 — The Federal Government has given an assurance that the country’s energy supply remains stable despite global uncertainties following conflicts in West Asia, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. “So far, the country’s energy supply remains at a stable level due to early planning and diversification of supply sources. “The government, through relevant ministries, is closely monitoring water supply together with water providers in each state to ensure sufficient supply.

items.title: Iran threatens Middle East infrastructure after Trump ultimatum
items.summary: TEHRAN: Iran on Sunday threatened to attack key infrastructure across the Middle East if US President Donald Trump follows through on his vow to "obliterate" the Islamic republic's power plants unless the Strait of Hormuz swiftly reopens. Trump, after signalling he may wind down the war, ratcheted up pressure on Iran's leadership, announcing a countdown over the Islamic republic's de facto blockade on the crucial trade route. Israel said it launched a fresh wave of strikes on the Iranian capital, Tehran, on Sunday in response.

items.title: China's premier vows to expand global 'trade pie': state media
items.summary: BEIJING: China's number two leader Li Qiang said Sunday that his country was willing to help expand the global "trade pie" by further opening up, state media reported, while he slammed unilateralism from certain countries. Many of China's key trading partners have increasingly called on Beijing to reduce its soaring trade surplus owing to its impact on local competition. China "will steadfastly advance high-level opening up, import more high-quality foreign goods, and work alongside all parties to promote the optimised and balanced development of trade", Premier Li Qiang told business executives in Beijing on Sunday, according to Xinhua.

items.title: Slovenia votes in tight polls, with conservatives eyeing comeback
items.summary: LJUBLJANA: Slovenians began voting on Sunday in tight parliamentary elections, with the conservatives of veteran politician Janez Janša, an admirer of US President Donald Trump, eyeing a comeback. A Janša return could see the ex-Yugoslav nation, a European Union member of two million people, take an illiberal turn again after four years of centre-left rule under liberal Prime Minister Robert Golob. Foreign interference claims have shaken the campaign, with authorities probing whether an Israeli intelligence firm was behind secretly recorded videos suggesting alleged graft in Golob's government.

items.title: Kelantan island folk take to the water for Raya reunions, grave visits
items.summary: During the festive season, those living in Pulau Beluru, Pulau Teluk Rejuna, and other nearby islets rely on boat services to cross the Kelantan River to reach the mainland. Many head toward Kota Baru, Pasir Mas, and other districts to celebrate the festivities with loved ones and pay respects at family graves. Self-employed Mohd Fadli Saudi from Pulau Teluk Rejuna said his journey during Ramadan carries a more solemn purpose.

items.title: Sarawak studies tougher rabies control as cat infections pose growing challenge
items.summary: KUCHING, March 22 — The Sarawak state government is studying the best approaches to strengthen efforts in controlling rabies, particularly infections among cats, said Sarawak Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Dr Sim Kui Hian. He said discussions have been held with the Sarawak State Health Department and the Department of Veterinary Services Sarawak to develop an integrated action plan. Dr Sim, who is also Sarawak’s Minister for Public Health, Housing and Local Government, reminded the public not to take the rabies threat lightly and to always take preventive measures, including ensuring their pets receive regular vaccinations.

items.title: Kelantan MB backs cost-saving Raya open houses, says use personal funds over public money
items.summary: PASIR MAS, March 22 — Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud expressed support for a cost-saving approach in organising open house events, including using personal funds without relying on government allocations. He said the approach of using one’s own expenses is in line with the government’s call for celebrations to be held in moderation. “For the state’s economy, open houses can sometimes be held, so we organise this using personal funds, while government events depend on the situation,” he said when met by reporters at his open house in Meranti here today, which was attended by about 5,000 guests.

items.title: Penang CM submits report for Anthony Loke on state quit rent hike, vows media silence
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 22 — Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow will prepare a report to be submitted to DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke regarding the implementation of new quit rent (land tax) rates, which came into effect on Jan 1 in the state. “I want to express my appreciation to the DAP secretary-general for arranging a meeting involving all three parties (Chow, Loke and former Penang CM Lim Guan Eng) to discuss the issue of the increase in quit rent rates in Penang, he said in a statement today. The revision of quit rent rates involves nearly 370,000 land titles throughout Penang, covering various land categories and uses, including residential, commercial, industrial, agricultural and other special categories.

items.title: 12 killed, dozens injured as bus collides with train in southeastern Bangladesh
items.summary: DHAKA: At least 12 people were killed and 26 others injured in a bus-train collision in the southeastern Comilla district early Sunday, fire service officials and police said, Anadolu Ajansi reported. A Dhaka-bound one-up train leaving port city Chittagong collided with a bus heading towards Chittagong from western Chapainawabganj district at the Paduar Bazar rail crossing area in Comilla city. The train dragged the bus one kilometre away.

items.title: Ramanan hails four Malaysian students for Bangkok innovation win
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri Ramanan Ramakrishnan has praised four Malaysian students for their success at the Bangkok Innovation Awards, where the team secured two gold medals and second place in the Primary School Engineering category. He said the achievement highlights the creative and high-impact ideas of the younger generation on the international stage. The four students are Ilaneyan Yuvarajan and Theerchanna Muniandy from SJKT KO Sarangapany, alongside Ilanarun Yuvarajan and Elamaaran Muniandy from SMK Sultan Badlishah.

items.title: Probe launched after video of police being provoked goes viral
items.summary: KUALA KRAI: Police are investigating alleged provocation against personnel involved in a Hari Raya operation on Friday night after videos of the incident went viral. Kuala Krai police chief Superintendent Mazlan Mamat said the footage shows members of the public provoking policemen during the 10pm operation aimed at ensuring public order. "Around midnight, police instructed the public to disperse and stop using banned firecrackers.

items.title: Why Malaysia still feels global oil shocks despite producing its own, Anwar explains
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 22 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia has not been spared from the recent surge in global oil prices following the conflict in West Asia, despite being an oil producing nation. He said the disruption at the Strait of Hormuz — the world’s main oil shipping route — has limited global supply and pushed prices higher. Nearly half of Malaysia’s oil supply passes through the strait, he noted.

items.title: Immigration Dept begins 24-hour monitoring of KL 'hotspots' for Hari Raya
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department has commenced 24-hour monitoring of foreigner "hotspots" in the federal capital to ensure public safety and order during the Aidilfitri festive season. Monitored locations include KLCC, TRX, SOGO, Pavilion, Bukit Bintang, Central Market, Petaling Street, Kotaraya, Masjid India, Chow Kit, and Kampung Baru. Affected routes include Jalan Ampang, Jalan Sultan Ismail, Jalan Imbi, Jalan Kia Peng, Jalan Changkat, Jalan Alor, Jalan Pudu, Jalan Silang, Jalan Tun HS Lee, Jalan Raja Laut, and Jalan Masjid India.

items.title: Contractor loses savings, company funds to fake investment scheme
items.summary: KUANTAN: A contractor lost RM356,342 in personal savings and company funds after falling victim to a fraudulent investment scheme. Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the 34-year-old victim encountered an investment advertisement on a website known as Marinasand on Feb 18. Between Feb 18 and March 19, the victim carried out 16 transactions to five different bank accounts involving RM356,342.

items.title: Samsuri calls for non-partisan council to tackle global economic impact
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Perikatan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar has called for a non-partisan approach to shield the country from the escalating global economic uncertainty caused by conflicts in the Middle East. Samsuri said the ongoing regional conflicts have begun to directly impact the world economy, including Malaysia, by driving up energy prices, disrupting supply chains, and straining global markets. "Malaysia needs a non-partisan approach that prioritises national interests over partisan politics, ensuring it is inclusive and action-oriented.

items.title: Man dies after falling into drain in Klang
items.summary: Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations centre chief Cik Hamdan Hamzah said the department received a distress call at 7.45am and dispatched a team to the scene. Firemen found the victim in the drain upon arrival. The victim was later pronounced dead, Cik Hamdan said when contacted.

items.title: Two more Malaysians arrested in Singapore over impersonation scams
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Two Malaysian men were arrested in Singapore yesterday for their suspected involvement in scams involving the impersonation of government officials. Singapore police said the arrests follow a surge in Malaysians travelling to the republic to assist scam syndicates in collecting cash and valuables from victims. The victim was duped into surrendering the gold at the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) building after scammers, posing as MAS and telecommunications officials, had claimed she was involved in a money laundering case.

items.title: China's Premier Li pledges national treatment for foreign companies
items.summary: BEIJING: Chinese Premier Li Qiang pledged on Sunday to improve the business environment and fully implement national treatment for foreign enterprises. • Li said China's competitive advantages stem from deepening reform and innovation, not subsidies and protection, crediting the hard work of Chinese people and businesses. • He said China will import more high-quality foreign goods and work with all parties to promote balanced trade development and expand the global trade pie.

items.title: Brothers nabbed stealing underground cables in Sungai Petani
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: Two brothers were arrested red-handed while attempting to steal underground cables near the clock tower at Jalan Tunku Ibrahim yesterday afternoon. Kuala Muda police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said officers spotted the men opening a manhole cover and climbing underground to cut the cables. The brothers has been taken to the Kuala Muda district police headquarters for further investigation.

items.title: Sri Lanka raises fuel prices by 25pct as war bites
items.summary: COLOMBO: Sri Lanka raised fuel prices by 25 per cent on Sunday, the second increase in two weeks, as the country prepared for more impact from the war in the Middle East. Regular petrol was increased to 398 rupees (US$1.30) per litre, up from 317 rupees, while diesel, the fuel commonly used for public transport, rose by 79 rupees to 382. Last week, the government ordered an eight percent increase in retail fuel prices and introduced rationing to limit consumption.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia still hit by global price surge despite oil production
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim says Malaysia is affected by global oil price fluctuations despite being a producer, as it imports more oil than it exports. "When this route is disrupted, global oil supply becomes limited, and prices surge. "Although Malaysia is an oil producer, we actually import more oil than we export," he said.

items.title: Coconut water sales soar as heatwave hits Padang Terap
items.summary: KUALA NERANG: The scorching heat in Padang Terap has led to a surge in demand for coconut water, with traders reporting rapid sales as temperatures hit 40°C. Coconut water trader Rosli Musa, 40, said he sold more than 10 coconuts in less than an hour today at his stall near the Bukit Tembaga junction. Rosli noted that the intense heat, which reportedly reached 40°C yesterday, has made outdoor conditions feel "like skin is burning."

items.title: Kenanga starts Southern Cable at "Outperform", sees grid upgrade upside
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: KENANGA Research has initiated coverage on Southern Cable Group Bhd, giving it an "Outperform" rating and a target price of RM2.71, based on 18 times financial year 2027 (FY27) earnings per share. The firm said it views Southern Cable as a key proxy to Malaysia's accelerating grid upgrade cycle. Kenanga Research said it projects Southern Cable's earnings to reach RM161 million in FY26 and RM196 million in FY27, reflecting year-on-year growth of 15 per cent and 22 per cent, respectively.

items.title: Juvenile whale found dead at Tanjung Aru Beach in KK days after release from Sutera Harbour jetty
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 22 — A juvenile whale previously spotted at the Sutera Harbour Resort jetty and released back into the sea was found dead at Tanjung Aru Beach near Shangri-La Tanjung Aru Resort, on Saturday. According to Info X, the carcass of the marine mammal was discovered stranded between rocks along the beach at about noon. Malaysian Civil Defence Force (APM) personnel on duty at the beach made the discovery.

items.title: Leaks, theft and ageing pipes: Fadillah vows bold push to fix Malaysia’s water woes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 22 — The issue of non-revenue water (NRW), which remains high in several states, including pipe leaks, water theft and ageing infrastructure, significantly impacts the efficiency of the country’s water supply system. “This includes investment in smart technology, holistic water resource management, and inter-agency cooperation to ensure the country’s water security is always guaranteed,” he said in a Facebook post in conjunction with World Water Day (WWD) celebrations today. Therefore, he said the Water Sector Transformation Roadmap 2040 (AIR 2040) also serves as Malaysia’s long-term plan to improve the country’s water management, ensure sufficient clean water supply, and make the water sector more modern and sustainable.

items.title: Sarawak firefighters extricate victim as Miri traffic light crash claims life and injures two others
items.summary: SIBU, March 22 — A woman was killed while two others were injured in a road accident involving a car and a four-wheel-drive (4WD) vehicle at the Vista Perdana traffic lights in Miri early this morning. The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) said the 23-year-old woman was pronounced dead at the scene by Ministry of Health (MOH) personnel. “Firefighters extricated the woman from a Perodua Bezza.

items.title: Three family members killed in Pulau Tioman motorcycle crash laid to rest in Muar cemetery
items.summary: MUAR, March 22 — Three family members, who were killed in a motorcycle accident on Jalan Tekek-Juara, Pulau Tioman, Pahang, last Friday, were laid to rest at the Batu 6 Muslim Cemetery, Bakri, at 12.15am today. The remains of Mohd Sabri Ja’afar, 42; his wife, Siti Aisyah Suhaime, 32; and their five-year-old son, Muhammad Yusuf Abdurrahman Mohd Sabri, arrived at Surau Ar-Rahman, Bakri, at about 10.45pm, before funeral prayers were performed by the congregation and family members. Rompin police chief Supt Sharif Shai Sharif Mondoi was reported to have said that the incident caused Mohd Sabri, who was a customer service officer at the National Water Research Institute of Malaysia, along with his wife and son, to die at the scene.

items.title: Maritime agency deploys boat and sets up base in Kuala Dungun after diver disappears off Pulau Tenggol
items.summary: Kemaman Maritime Zone director Maritime Commander Abdul Halim Hamzah said the incident occurred at 4.30pm when Tam Peng Hoong, 66, was diving in the area with his son. He said the Maritime Rescue Sub Centre (MRSC) Kuantan, Pahang, received a report on the incident this afternoon, and a search and rescue (SAR) operation was activated at 2.10pm, nine minutes after the report was received. The SAR operation will resume Sunday morning,” he said.

items.title: Second teenage boy found drowned at Sungai Plan Lot waterfall in Bintulu after swimming with friends
items.summary: SIBU, March 22 — Another teenage boy who was reported missing while swimming with friends at the Sungai Plan Lot waterfall, Jalan Permata, Kidurong, Bintulu, yesterday, was found drowned late last night. The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) said that the victim’s body, identified as Darren Dakun Paruscrum, 12, was found by members of the public at 11.19pm, about five metres from the riverbank. The body was later handed over to the police for further action,” Sarawak JBPM said in a statement.

items.title: Police issue 19 traffic summonses during two-night crime prevention operation in Bau
items.summary: KUCHING, March 22 — A total of 48 vehicles were inspected and 19 traffic summonses issued during a crime prevention operation conducted over two nights from March 20 to 21. The integrated operation, known as Op Bersepadu Pencegahan Jenayah, was carried out from 10pm on March 20 until 3am the following day, covering Bau town, the residential areas in Bau and Jambusan, and a roadblock at KM2 on Jalan Bau–Serikin. According to the Bau District Police Headquarters (IPD Bau) in a Facebook post, 48 vehicles and 60 individuals were checked during the operation.

items.title: Indonesian consul highlights growing travel and economic ties with Sarawak amid undocumented worker concerns
items.summary: KUCHING, March 22 — Increasing cross-border movement between Sarawak and Indonesia is driving economic and social exchange, but also bringing persistent challenges such as undocumented migration, says Indonesia’s Consul General in Kuching Abdullah Zulkifli. He said the recent holiday period in Indonesia from March 18 to 24 saw many Indonesians travelling long distances to Sarawak, including some who journeyed by land for over 20 hours via Pontianak before reaching Kuching. Despite these positive developments, Abdullah acknowledged ongoing challenges involving Indonesian migrant workers in Sarawak, particularly those entering or working without proper documentation.

items.title: Teen girl allegedly raped by new acquaintance in Maran, suspect arrested
items.summary: Maran district police chief Superintendent Wong Kim Wai said a report was lodged by the victim’s mother regarding the incident. According to the report, the alleged incident took place in early March at a house in Maran when the victim went out with the suspect. Following the report, a team of officers from the Criminal Investigation Division of the Maran district police headquarters arrested the suspect on Friday for further investigation He said police are conducting further investigations under Section 376 of the Penal Code to gather evidence related to the case.

items.title: Kelantan police tighten border watch, reminding Hari Raya travellers to cross legally at authorised ICQS checkpoints
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, March 22 — Police have advised locals living along Sungai Golok along the Malaysia-Thailand border to use legal routes if they wish to visit relatives in conjunction with Aidilfitri. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said police are looking into the matter seriously due to the possibility of an increase in illegal cross-border movement, especially as water levels in Sungai Golok have become increasingly shallow. Earlier, Rantau Panjang Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) deputy commander Bakri Sulaiman said about 50,000 travellers are expected to use the Rantau Panjang ICQS complex within three days before and three days after Hari Raya Aidilfitri.

items.title: Scorching days ahead as MetMalaysia flags heatwave in Kedah, with Penang, Perak and Negeri Sembilan on alert
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 22 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) today issued a Level 2 (heatwave) warning for Padang Terap, Kedah. MetMalaysia, in a statement issued at 6am today, said that 12 areas in Peninsular Malaysia are at Level 1 (alert), involving Perlis and Kedah (Baling, Sik, Pendang, Pokok Sena, Kubang Pasu, Langkawi Island, Kuala Muda and Bandar Baharu). According to the agency, the heatwave level refers to a daily maximum temperature of 37 to 40 degrees Celsius for at least three consecutive days, while the alert level refers to a daily maximum temperature of 35 to 37 degrees Celsius for at least three consecutive days.

items.title: Regent of Johor throws open palace doors, Singapore ministers cross Causeway for Hari Raya celebration
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 22 — Two Singapore Cabinet ministers attended a Hari Raya Aidilfitri Open House hosted by the Regent of Johor, Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim, at Istana Bukit Serene on Saturday, reaffirming strong cross-border ties and cooperation. Social and Family Development Minister Masagos Zulkifli and Education Minister Desmond Lee, together with their spouses, conveyed their Hari Raya greetings to His Royal Highness the Tunku Mahkota of Johor and Her Highness Che’ Puan Mahkota Khaleeda of Johor. “This visit continues the annual tradition of Singapore’s leaders attending the Johor royal family’s Hari Raya Open House,” the statement read.

items.title: How an AI-driven pedestrian system is making school crossings safer at SMK Puchong Permai (VIDEO)
items.summary: PUCHONG, March 22 — Just before the morning bell at SMK Puchong Permai, groups of students gather at a pedestrian crossing along the busy road outside their school, chatting and greeting one another as they wait for the light to turn green. The crossing is now overseen by a newly installed smart pedestrian safety system known as Sensa, an initiative by the Subang Jaya City Council (MBSJ) aimed at strengthening road safety in school zones and pedestrian areas with heavy traffic. Road bumps have also been installed around the school, with two on one side and one on the other, to slow down traffic near the pedestrian crossing.

items.title: Search continues for second boy after tragic drowning at Bintulu waterfall
items.summary: SIBU, March 21 — A 12-year-old boy drowned, while his 13-year-old friend remains missing after they went swimming at the Sungai Plan Lot waterfall in Kidurong, Bintulu, this afternoon. The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) identified the deceased as Tristan Mariss Marissa Erna, whose body was recovered at 5.20 pm. A JBPM spokesman said the victim was found five metres from the riverbank, in waters estimated to be nine metres deep.

items.title: New Chongqing–Kota Kinabalu route expected to spur Sabah tourism during Visit Malaysia 2026
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 21 — Sabah welcomed its first direct flight from Chongqing on Thursday with the arrival of Chongqing Airlines’ inaugural service, linking the southwestern China city to Kota Kinabalu. Chongqing Airlines, a subsidiary of China Southern Airlines, operated the inaugural flight, which carried 154 passengers. STB chief executive officer Julinus Jeffery Jimit, Tourism Malaysia Sabah director Haryanty Abu Bakar, and China Southern Airlines Kuala Lumpur Office general manager Guo Hao welcomed the flight upon arrival.

items.title: Lim Guan Eng pauses public criticism, welcomes meeting with Anthony Loke and Chow Kon Yeow on Penang quit rent dispute
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 21 — DAP veteran Lim Guan Eng has welcomed party secretary‑general Anthony Loke Siew Fook’s move to convene a meeting between him and Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow to resolve the ongoing dispute over the state’s steep quit rent increases. “I wish to thank DAP Secretary‑General Loke Siew Fook for his concern and initiative to convene a meeting involving Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow and myself to find a satisfactory resolution over the contentious hike in quit rent in Penang this year, some by more than 3,000 times,” he said. Lim said his Bagan parliamentary office and Air Putih state assemblyperson office would continue assisting affected landowners with appeal submissions, noting that both offices had helped about 800 applicants so far.

items.title: Man injured in Kulim after suspect fires revolver during late‑night dispute
items.summary: Kulim deputy police chief Deputy Superintendent Tengku Mohd Faisal Tengku Yeng said the confrontation began when the victim was stopped by a 42‑year‑old man who had earlier asked him to come to his home in Kampung Naga Lilit. Police later arrested the suspect, a lorry driver, in front of his house in Kampung Naga Lilit. Police said the suspect has 11 previous criminal cases and three drug‑related offences, while the victim has six narcotics cases and several other criminal entries.

items.title: Sarawak records third rabies death of the year after 10‑year‑old Kuching boy bitten by stray dog
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 21 — A 10‑year‑old boy from Kuching has died of rabies, marking Sarawak’s third fatality from the disease this year. The department said the boy had cleaned the wound himself at home, which meant he did not receive the immediate medical care required after a suspected rabies exposure. It said laboratory tests confirmed rabies infection on March 14 and that the boy died four days later on March 18.

items.title: International Forest Day 2026: Malaysia cuts logging limit under MP13 to protect forests
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 21 — The government continues to strengthen its commitment to Sustainable Forest Management (SFM) by setting the Annual Allowable Cut (CTT) in Permanent Reserved Forests (HSK) for the 13th Malaysia Plan (MP13) at an area of 890,000 hectares (ha), compared to 1,165,300 ha during the 12th Malaysia Plan (MP12). “This simultaneously demonstrates a positive economic impact through sustainable forest management activities that include forest product extraction and logging,” he said in a statement here today in conjunction with International Day of Forests. Arthur said the Ministry of Natural Resources and Environment (NRES) through the Peninsular Malaysia Forestry Department, Sabah Forestry Department, Sarawak Forestry Department, and state forestry departments are actively empowering forest-based ecotourism areas that have unique natural characteristics.

items.title: Kelantan JPJ denies role in fatal crash involving journalist’s son, says viral video of lorry chase is false
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, March 21 — The Kelantan Road Transport Department (JPJ) has refuted viral social media allegations linking one of its enforcement vehicles to a fatal accident at KM 85.5 of Jalan Kota Bharu-Machang-Jeli near Kampung Rahmat, Bukit Bunga, yesterday. Kelantan JPJ director Mohd Misuari Abdullah said the department takes the spread of inaccurate information regarding the incident seriously. “The claim that the accident was caused by JPJ personnel chasing a lorry, as depicted in a 34-second viral video, is false and baseless.

items.title: Musa Aman and Hajiji Noor attend Aidilfitri prayers at Sabah State Mosque
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 21 — Head of State Tun Musa Aman, accompanied by Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, performed Aidilfitri prayers with thousands of congregants at the State Mosque today. Upon arrival, Musa was received by Hajiji, Speaker Datuk Seri Kadzim M Yahya, Deputy Chief Minister Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun, Local Government and Housing Minister and Main Committee for Islamic Celebrations Chairman Datuk Mohd Arifin Arif, State Secretary Datuk Seri Safar Untong, and Sabah Federal Secretary Datuk Mohd Razali Wahab. — Daily Express
